text: All utilities are on and appear to be working properly. All kitchen appliances fixed to the property appear to in working order. The subject's  and crawl space area has been inspected. There is attic access. However, the scuttle was too high to reach. The roof appears to be adequately vented. The subject meets all FHA/HUD minimum guidelines as outlined by Handbook 4000.1 and the applicable Mortgagee Letter 09-28, except: Scrape & paint exterior window frames on enclosed porch, shed & back door frame. All paint chips should be removed from the area to ensure safety.

All utilities are on and appear to be working properly. All kitchen appliances fixed to the property appear to be in working order. An attempt was made to inspect the subject's crawl space, but the entrances were inaccessible at the time of inspection. Photos have been included in this report of the crawl space access, it appears to be properly ventilated. An attempt was made to inspect the subject's attic, but the attic access was painted over and made inaccessible at the time of inspection. A photo of the attic access has been included in this report. A sample photo of peeling paint on the exterior window frames have been included, however, it appears that the window frame was painted after 1978. A visual inspection of the subject's roof was performed. No missing shingles or active leaks were visible and it appears that no immediate require repairs are necessary. It holds physical depreciation that is consistent with roofs of a similar age. The subject meets all FHA/HUD miniumum guidelines as outling by Handbook 4000.1 and the applicable Mortagee Letter 09-28.

All utilities are on and appear to be working properly. All kitchen appliances fixed to the property appear to be in working order. An attempt was made to inspect the subject's attic, but the attic access was hindered by insulation during the time of observation. Photos of the attic and its access point have been included in this report. The subject's basement  and foundation were observed and no visible signs of necessary repair were identified. It holds similar physical depreciation with foundations of a similar age. A visual inspection of the subject's roof was performed. No missing shingles or active leaks were visible and it appears that no immediate required repairs are necessary. It holds physical depreciation that is consistent with roofs of a similar age. The subject meets all FHA/HUD miniumum guidelines as outling by Handbook 4000.1 and the applicable Mortagee Letter 09-28.

All utilities are on and appear to be working properly. All kitchen appliances fixed to the property appear to be in working order. A visual inspection of the subject's attic was performed and a photo has been included in this report. A visual inspection of the subject's crawl space was performed and a photo has been included in this report.  The subject's foundation did not appear to have any necessary immediate repairs, however, the appraiser lacks the qualifications to be considered an expert in foundation repair. A visual inspection of the subject's roof was performed. No missing shingles or active leaks were visible and it appears that no immediate required repairs are necessary. It holds physical depreciation that is consistent with roofs of a similar age. The Residential Real Property Disclosure Report states, "Used fireplace once when first bought house, tried once since and it does not stay on or lit (electric fireplace). The subject meets all FHA/HUD miniumum guidelines as outling by Handbook 4000.1 and the applicable Mortagee Letter 09-28 except for the following repairs: the installation of a handrailing in the stairwell to second floor and secure loose railing in stairwell to basement in order to minimize potential safety hazards.

All utilities are on and appear to be working properly. The appraiser has adhered to ANSI standards in measuring the subject property. The seller's Residential Real Property Disclosure Report indicates a history of concerns regarding the potability of the property's drinking water. According to the seller, the water was not considered safe for drinking at the time of occupancy approximately 11 years ago. While the seller states conditions have improved, the water has not been recently tested, and the occupants reportedly do not use the water for drinking purposes. The appraiser is not qualified to test or evaluate water quality and has not performed any such testing. This appraisal assumes the subject's water supply is typical for the market area unless otherwise determined by a qualified professional.

All utilities are on and appear to be working properly. All kitchen appliances fixed to the property appear to be in working order. A visual inspection of the subject's attic was performed and a photo has been included in this report.A visual inspection of the subject's roof was performed. No missing shingles or active leaks were visible and it appears that no immediate required repairs are necessary. It holds physical depreciation that is consistent with roofs of a similar age. The subject meets all FHA/HUD miniumum guidelines as outling by Handbook 4000.1 and the applicable Mortagee Letter 09-28. Additionally, the subject utilizes a private septic system. This aspect is typical for the market area and does not have a significant impact on the marketability of the subject.   Underwriter Conditions  please add additional parcel to appraisal  -Per Residential Sales Contract Change Addendum signed 01/14/2026, this appraisal has been updated to reflect the inclusion of parcel #13-13-09-204-006 in the sale of the subject.  they have removed the additional parcel from the contract and will not be including it - could you please correct back to just one parcel ID and adjust value to original report?  - Additional parcel has been removed from the appraisal. The report has been edited back to the original version and the estimate of value has returned to $290,000.   1500 seller credit added today - please add to report  -The report has been updated to reflect the additional $1,500 in seller credit.

All utilities are on and appear to be working properly. The appraiser has adhered to ANSI standards in measuring the subject property. The disclosure report in the contract indicates that there is material defects and recurring leakage problems in the basement. A visual inspection of the foundation was performed and no visible signs of significant deterioration or leakage problems were identified, however, the appraiser does not have the required certification to be considered an expert on foundation condition and repairs.

All utilities are on and appear to be working properly. The appraiser has adhered to ANSI standards in measuring the subject property. A visual inspection was performed of the subject's foundation and it appears that there are no areas of physical deterioration affecting the structural integrity of the house. It appears that concrete block has been used to reinforce areas of the brick foundation where previously necessary. However, the appraiser lacks the qualifications to be considered an expert on foundation condition and necessary repairs.

All utilities are on and appear to be working properly. The appraiser has adhered to ANSI standards in measuring the subject property. A visual inspection of the subject's foundation was performed and no signs of necessary repairs were visible. However, the appraiser does not have the necessary qualifications to be considered an expert in foundation maintenance and repair. The seller has indicated in the property disclosure report that they are aware of recurring leakage in the crawl space. It was raining during the inspection of the subject and the photo of the crawl space has depicted water in the crawl space area.

All utilities are on and appear to be working properly. The subject will utilize a private septic system. This aspect is typical for houses of a similar location and does not appear to significantly impact the marketability of the subject. As the dimensions on the subject's blueprint were illegible, copies of the main floor and crawl space plans have been included in this report in place of a sketch.
